description_long: This object is a pair of pruning shears or garden pliers, featuring two elongated, slightly curved handles made of black material, likely plastic or rubber, for ergonomic grip. The handles are joined by a central metallic pivot mechanism, which allows the two arms to open and close smoothly. The jaws are robust and curved, designed for cutting through plant stems or small branches, with a sharp, metallic cutting edge visible along one side. The tool has a compact, streamlined profile, with the cutting blade and anvil forming a scissor-like action. Subtle yellow accents are present near the pivot, possibly for added grip or visual distinction. The overall construction suggests durability and comfort for repeated manual use in gardening or light urban maintenance tasks.
description: A pair of black, ergonomic pruning shears with curved handles and a sharp, metallic cutting jaw, joined by a central pivot. Yellow accents highlight the grip area near the joint.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the curved handles and the sharp, curved cutting jaw.
description_view_1: Front view displaying the open jaws and the ergonomic curvature of the handles.
description_view_2: Opposite side view highlighting the metallic blade and the streamlined profile of the handles.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the back of the jaws and the inner contours of the handles.
attribute_handle_type: Curved, ergonomic handles with black rubberized or plastic coating for comfort and grip.
attribute_jaw_design: Curved, bypass-style jaws with a sharp cutting blade and anvil, designed for clean cuts through plant material.
attribute_pivot_mechanism: Fixed central metallic pivot, providing smooth scissor-like action without adjustable jaw width.
attribute_grip_pattern: Smooth with subtle texturing, likely rubberized for enhanced grip and slip resistance.
